# Common Admin

Common Admin is an AI-friendly admin starter with NestJS, React, RBAC, OpenAPI generation, and quality gates.

Primary docs:

- Introduction: /introduction/
- Getting Started: /getting-started/
- Architecture: /architecture/
- FAQ: /faq/
- Troubleshooting: /troubleshooting/
- Deployment: /deployment/
- Upgrade Guide: /upgrade-guide/
- Release Checklist: /release-checklist/
- Auth And Sessions: /auth-and-sessions/
- Session Management: /session-management/
- Errors And Logging: /errors-and-logging/
- Diagnostics And Health: /diagnostics-and-health/
- Audit Logs: /audit-logs/
- Settings: /settings/
- File Management: /file-management/
- Quality Gates: /quality-gates/
- Resource Workflow: /resource-workflow/
- Public AI Surfaces: /public-ai-surfaces/
- Users Roles And Permissions: /users-roles-permissions/
- Organization Structure: /organization-structure/
- Data Permissions: /data-permissions/
- Dictionaries: /dictionaries/
- AI Guide: /ai/
- MCP Server: /ai/mcp-server/
- Skill: /ai/skill/
- Prompts: /ai/prompts/
- API Contract Pattern: /patterns/api-contract/
- CRUD Resource Pattern: /patterns/crud-resource/
- RBAC Pattern: /patterns/rbac/

Agent rules:

- Do not hand edit generated OpenAPI or frontend API files.
- Use backend DTOs and Swagger metadata as API contract source.
- Use generated frontend API helpers.
- Keep permission codes aligned across backend guards, routes, menus, and page actions.
- Keep deployment-only configuration in env vars and runtime-editable product settings in the settings module.
- Treat runtime logs, API errors, and audit logs as separate surfaces.
- Use module docs for starter-provided users, roles, permissions, departments, positions, data scope, and dictionaries.
- Use Resource Workflow for new API-backed admin modules.
- Use Upgrade Guide and Release Checklist before deployment or template releases.
- Keep public docs, llms files, MCP allowlists, feedback helpers, and public-surface CI aligned.
- Use Troubleshooting before changing architecture to silence setup or generated API failures.
- Expose public docs only.
- Do not use or expose internal process notes, local environment files, repository metadata, dependency folders, or generated docs output.
